full_response:
Let the two 3 × 3 matrices be  

 [M] = (M_{ij}), [N] = (N_{ij}),  i,j = 1,2,3.  

To write the product [N]·[M] in Σ-notation we proceed as follows.

1. Definition of a matrix product  
   The element in the i-th row and j-th column of the product matrix [N][M] is obtained by taking the dot-product of the i-th row of N with the j-th column of M.

2. Σ-notation for that element  
   ( [N][M] )_{ij} = Σ_{k=1}^{3} N_{ik}\,M_{k j}.  

3. Collecting all elements, the product matrix is therefore  

 [N][M] = ( Σ_{k=1}^{3} N_{ik} M_{k j} )_{i,j=1}^{3}.  

(If one also wants to include the given vectors for completeness:  
 (v M)_j = Σ_{i=1}^{3} v_i M_{ij},  (N u)_i = Σ_{j=1}^{3} N_{ij} u_j.)

\boxed{(NM)_{ij} \;=\; \displaystyle\sum_{k=1}^{3} N_{ik}\,M_{kj}\,,\qquad i,j=1,2,3}  

Confidence: 91%
